observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant comply with the reporting requirement and prepare the schedules pertaining to the foregoing accounts to support the balance presented in the financial statements.

We recommended that management immediately correct the noted deficiencies to ensure the safety of the occupants of the Buildings owned and prevent the imposition of fines, penalties, abatements or closures.

2.) The Accounting Unit to prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement of the PNB accounts and make the necessary adjustments, as it warrants; and 

3.) The Accounting Unit and the Office of the Municipal Treasurer to undertake the necessary reconciliation of records.

3.) Municipal Accountant and GSO/Municipal Treasurer maintain ledgers and stock and property cards, respectively. Also, maintain a PPE Register to facilitate the conduct of year-end physical inventory, the municipality use this as basis in the conduct and reconciliation of the accounts; and

4.) Submit a copy of the RPCPPE to the Office of the Auditor not later than January 31st of each year as provided in Volume II of the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) to facilitate verification of the inventory report.

We recommended and the management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the appropriate adjusting entry to reclassify the undeposited collections from Cash – Local Treasury to the appropriate receivable account.

We recommended and the management agreed to direct the Office of the Municipal Accountant to locate the disbursement vouchers and supporting documents for the unrecorded disbursements and to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to record the reconciling items in the books of accounts of the LGU and to submit to the Auditor a copy of the Journal Entry Voucher taking up the adjustments.

We recommended and the management agreed to direct the concerned officials to fully liquidate the balance of the petty cash fund in accordance with the provisions of the manual and issue a demand letter to accountable officers who are no longer connected with the LGU.

We recommended and the management agreed to refrain from granting additional cash advance to concerned officials and employees unless the previous cash advance granted to them have been liquidated and require all officials and employees to liquidate their cash advances immediately and institute appropriate legal sanction against concerned officials or employees, by withholding or suspension of their salaries.

6.6	We recommended and the management agreed to:
Direct the Municipal Accountant in coordination with the Office of the Municipal Engineer to retrieve the certificates of completion of all items of PPE where no depreciation expense was provided;
Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of the depreciation of PPE accounts, and record the adjustment; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain the required Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Card (PPELC).

We recommended and the management agreed to direct the Office of the Municipal Accountant to review available records or documents to prepare the proper schedule; monitor the status of unsettled receivables; and demand or enforce collection from persons/entities liable.

We recommended and the management agreed that the Office of the Municipal Accountant submit first notice of compliance to the Office of the Auditor for the issuance of NSSDC before dropping the recorded disallowances from the books of accounts. For amounts already credited, submit notice of compliance to the Office of the Auditor for the issuance of NSSDC and for the reconciliation of records.

We recommended and the management agreed to direct the Office of the Municipal Accountant to:
Review/verify available records or documents and prepare the proper schedule to determine the expense portion of the prepayments and the reason for the abnormal balances; and
Record the appropriate adjusting and/or correcting entries to effect the results of review/verification.

We recommended and the management agreed that the Office of the Municipal Accountant:
Conduct a thorough analysis in coordination with the Office of the Municipal Engineer to determine which among the projects in the construction in progress accounts are completed or not;
Reclassify the completed project into its appropriate asset account and submit the corresponding journal entry voucher (JEV) to the Office of the Auditor; and
Maintain index cards for each type of construction in progress account containing relevant information such as but not limited to (name of project, location, funding source, total project cost, duration of the project, total cost incurred to date & progress billing) for monitoring purposes.

We recommended and the management agreed that that the Municipal Accountant comply with the reporting requirement and prepare the schedules pertaining to the foregoing accounts to support the balance presented in the financial statements.

We recommended and the management agreed to:
Ensure that the needed drugs and medicines be procured are listed in the updated PNDF. If those were not listed, request for an exemption from PNDF addressed to DOH prior to procurement.
Use the updated DPRI on a timely basis in setting prices for the needed drugs and medicines in the preparation of PPMP, ABC and PRs. If no DPRI is available on the needed drugs, a previously awarded ABCs for the related items or market survey should be made by the procuring unit.
Ensure that the PPMP and PRs include shelf-life requirement, as well as technical specifications of drugs and medicines to be procured to avoid unwanted deliveries.
Avoid referring to brand names when procuring drugs and medicines, and strictly follow the guidelines prescribed in the IRR of RA No. 9184.

We recommended and the management agreed to strictly observe the guidelines provided in the COA Circular No. 2012-002 and NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 in utilizing its LDRRM Funds.

We recommended and the management agreed to:
1. Submit an updated/revised LDRRMFIP to include the revised PPAs funded under the unexpended LDRRMF of prior years, and under the LDRRMF of the current year; and
2.  Strictly adhere to the guidelines provided in the Circular.

We recommended and the management agreed that:
The GFPS include in their Annual GPB the attendance to seminars on Gender Mainstreaming Evaluation Framework (GMEF) and Gender Responsive LGU (GeRL) Assessment Tool to capacitate themselves in the conduct of internal gender audit.
The GFPS conduct gender audit on its agency thru the use of GMEF and GeRL Assessment Tool.

We recommended and the management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant and/or the Municipal Budget Officer to create a separate Responsibility Center (RC) for their GFPS to facilitate the generation of all reports pertaining to all GAD related financial transactions and to comply the requirements set forth in the COA Circular No. 2021-008.  Moving forward, use this RC in the recording of all GAD related transactions.

We recommended and the management agreed to require the Office of the Municipal Treasurer to timely furnish the office of the Municipal Accountant the duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year to properly establish the RPT/SET Receivable at the beginning of the year.

We recommended and the management agreed that:
The Municipal Treasurer exhaust all means to enforce in their locality the collection of RPT/SET against the taxpayers such as but not limited to sending demand letters in a regular manner highlighting the consequences of non-payment of taxes and the next possible action the taxing authority can do; and
The Municipal Treasurer to proactively forward the list of delinquent taxpayers to the Provincial Taxing authority and coordinate with them the imposition/implementation of next higher remedies in collecting taxes where they have the power/authority such as distraint of personal property or levy on real property.

We recommended and the management agreed that:
The Waterworks System Collector with the supervision of the Municipal Treasurer, regularly provide the list of consumers who are “Due For Disconnection” to the meter reader/disconnector and advise them to strictly implement the corresponding sanctions;
The Municipal Treasurer direct the water collector to prepare notice of disconnection to all users with 2 months arrears to enforce payment and non-disconnection thereof; and
The Municipal Treasurer forward a copy of the list of delinquencies to the Municipal Accountant and the latter to draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) taking up a receivable and income accounts for the delinquencies from waterworks system charges.

We recommended and the management agreed through the Local School Board to correct the abovementioned deficiencies and strictly comply with the guidelines provided in Circular in relation to the preparation and approval of the SEF Budget.

We recommended and the management agreed that:
The Municipal Mayor direct the appropriate office to submit the list of all on-going government PPA and those that are to be implemented during the year and to inform the SA and ATL within ten days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are already posted;
The appropriate office as directed by the Municipal Mayor prepare and submit monthly monitoring report on project status; and
The Office of the Municipal Engineer ensure that the project status in the project signboard is maintained and kept current as possible and updated in accordance with COA Circular 2013-004.

We recommended and the management agreed to:
Direct the Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the General Services Officer to comply with the Property Insurance Law in insuring all of its serviceable properties with the GSIS, and follow the guidelines prescribed in COA Circular No. 2018-002; and
Direct the Municipal Budget Officer to include in its proposed annual budget in the subsequent year the premiums of such insurance coverage with the GSIS. In case of budget constraints, the MBO may charge such appropriation to the LDRRM Fund of the LGU.

We recommended and the management agreed that the Municipal Vice Mayor and the Municipal Accountant be included in the list of bondable public officers of the LGU and be properly bonded with the Bureau of the Treasury.

We recommended and the management agreed to strictly observe the guidelines on the monetization of leave credits as provided in the Rule XVI of the Omnibus Rules Implementing Book V of Executive Order (EO) No. 292, and exercise due diligence in granting monetization.

We recommended that management require all officials and employees to liquidate their cash advances immediately and institute appropriate legal sanction against concerned officials or employees, by withholding or suspension of their salaries.

We recommended that the non-existing or missing PPE items without available record of accountability be identified and be derecognized from the books of accounts in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006 for the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts of government agencies to establish PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability.

a. The Municipal Mayor consider the designation or appointment of a GSO subject to the limitations of existing laws rules and regulations; and

b. The designated or appointed GSO regularly renew/update the PARs of the PPEs every three years or as soon as there is change in custody or accountability to strengthen control and accountability over government assets.
We recommended that the Office of the Municipal Accountant prepare the corresponding adjusting entry to correct the erroneous recording of prior years’ accounts payable and other affected accounts
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant strictly observe the provisions of the IPSAS in the preparation of disclosures in the NFS

We recommended that the LGU together with the Solid Waste Management Board establish a MRF and/or Residual Collection Area (RCA) in accordance with the requirements of RA No. 9003 in order to properly implement the provisions of the Solid Waste Management Act.
We recommended that the Municipality perform its duties and functions in accordance with the approved SWMP in order to properly implement the mandate of the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act.

We recommended that Management observe the modes of procurement provided under the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 even in transactions which are immediately necessary. All procurement activities must be in accordance with the specific law, rules and regulations.

We recommended that Management observe the modes of procurement provided under the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and other issuances of the GPPB even in transactions which are immediately necessary during a state of calamity. All procurement activities must be in accordance with the specific law, rules and regulations.

We recommended that Management procure supplies, materials and equipment directly from the PS or secure the CNAS before procuring from local suppliers. For items not available from the PS, as shown on its website, printed form of the webpage or a screenshot of the same showing the date and the time it was accessed must be secured first before procuring the CSE items from other sources.
We recommended that Management grant honoraria to BAC members based on successfully completed procurement projects following the rates provided under DBM Budget Circular 2004-5A.

We recommended that Management observe the provisions of EO No. 77 and DILG MC No. 2019-82 in the approval of official local travels and the Municipal Accountant ensure the completeness and propriety of supporting documents attached to claims for travelling expenses.

We recommended that the Office of the Municipal Accountant submit first notice of compliance to the Office of the Auditor for the issuance of NSSDC before dropping the recorded disallowances from the books of accounts. For amounts already credited, submit notice of compliance to the Office of the Auditor for the issuance of NSSDC and for the reconciliation of records.

1.) Set-up depreciation expense for the corresponding years where depreciation was not recognized and draw the corresponding JEV to effect the adjustments in the ensuing year; and

 2.) Maintain PPELC of each PPE to record acquisition, description, custody, estimated useful life, depreciation, disposal and other information about the PPE.

1.) Provide notice of deliveries within twenty-four (24) hours from deliveries of goods/items delivered; and 

2.) Submit Report of Inspection of Consumables within twenty four (24) hours.
We recommended that management implement optimally the PPAs contained in the LDRRMF Investment Plan.

We recommended that management require all officials and employees to liquidate their cash advances immediately and institute appropriate legal sanction against concerned officials or employees, by withholding or suspension of their salaries.

We recommended that the non-existing or missing PPE items without available record of accountability be identified and be derecognized from the books of accounts in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006 for the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts of government agencies to establish PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability.

We recommended that:
The Municipal Mayor consider the designation or appointment of a GSO subject to the limitations of existing laws rules and regulations; and
The designated or appointed GSO regularly renew/update the PARs of the PPEs every three years or as soon as there is change in custody or accountability to strengthen control and accountability over government assets.
We recommended that the Office of the Municipal Accountant prepare the corresponding adjusting entry to correct the erroneous recording of prior years’ accounts payable and other affected accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant comply with the reporting requirement and prepare the schedules pertaining to the foregoing accounts to support the balance presented in the financial statements.
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant strictly observe the provisions of the IPSAS in the preparation of disclosures in the NFS.

We recommended that management comply with the reporting guidelines of COA Circular No. 2015-008, as follows:
The General Services Officer, at the end of the accounting period, render a Report on Local Road Network;
The Municipal Accountant disclose in the Notes to the Financial Statements the total road network system; and
The Inventory Committee prepare the Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System.

We recommended that management immediately correct the noted deficiencies to ensure the safety of the occupants of the Buildings owned and prevent the imposition of fines, penalties, abatements or closures.

We recommended that the LGU together with the Solid Waste Management Board establish a MRF and/or Residual Collection Area (RCA) in accordance with the requirements of RA No. 9003 in order to properly implement the provisions of the Solid Waste Management Act.
We recommended that the Municipality perform its duties and functions in accordance with the approved SWMP in order to properly implement the mandate of the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act.

We recommended that the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) with the help of the Municipal Planning and Development Office to:
Institutionalize the GAD database with adequate and systematically gathered sex-disaggregated data which will provide the bases for gender analysis, planning, programming and policy formulation;
Undertake gender analysis to identify and confirm existing gender issues and ensure that the programs, projects and activities are responsive to the said issues; and
Revisit Annex B of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01, which identifies the data requirements for particular gender issues.

We recommended that:
The management establish GAD monitoring and evaluation system to effectively and efficiently monitor GAD implemented projects; and
The Municipal Mayor issue an Executive Order creating the GAD M&E Team as required under Section 3.4 and 5.1 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 so that GAD implemented projects will be religiously monitored and evaluated.

We recommended the following:
Prepare a Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) clearly stating the objective, responsibilities and accountabilities of both parties;
Evaluate the efficiency and effectiveness of the livelihood projects based on the rights, obligations and objectives laid down in the MOA; and
Keep proper and complete documentations to support the implementation and evaluation of the livelihood projects.

We recommended the following:
The Municipal Mayor direct the appropriate office to inform the SA and ATL within ten (10) days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are already posted;
The appropriate office as directed by the Municipal Mayor prepare and submit monthly monitoring report on project status; and
The Office of the Municipal Engineer evaluate the maintenance of project status in the project signboard as current as possible and updated in accordance with COA Circular 2013-004.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor with the assistance of the Municipal Engineer:
Issue an executive order creating the CPES – IU, identifying its members and responsibilities or integrate the CPEs function in an existing unit with similar functions which shall act as the CPES – IU; and
Formulate and adopt specific Implementing Guidelines for the evaluation of project types which do not have specific CPES Guidelines.

We recommended the following:
The Municipal Treasurer, through the designated remittance in-charge, if any, to be more vigilant of the payment schedules on the timely remittance of the mandatory PhilHealth contributions;
Ensure that checks drawn for the payment of contribution be delivered immediately or on earliest time possible to the PhilHealth Office before the deadline to consider all possible contingencies that may arise and establish further monitoring of the same; and
The Municipal Mayor, through the Municipal Accountant, order the refund of the interest charged to the Municipality amounting to ₱2,323.40 by the erring accountable official concerned and to refrain from charging the penalties/interests to the LGU funds as it is personal in nature.

We recommended the following:
The Municipal Accountant submit the lacking DTRs and Official Receipts to the Office of the Auditor for validation;
The Disbursing Officer ensure that every employee affixed his/her signature on the payroll to acknowledge receipt, or require authorization letter in case the payee cannot claim personally his/her salary/wage before tendering payment; and
The Municipal Accountant thoroughly review the payrolls submitted to ensure that they are properly supported with necessary documents as provided under the Circular before its approval and liquidation.

We recommended the following:
Submit to the Office of the Auditor supplemental or revised APP integrating the procurement of motor vehicles;
Ensure that the necessary authorization from appropriate authority shall be secured first prior to the start of procurement process; and
Ensure that subsequent procurement of motor vehicle is consistent with the centralized procurement through the Procurement Service (PS).

We recommended that the BAC:
Use Direct Retail Purchase method in the procurement of its fuel, oil and lubricants and include the estimated amounts to be procured under such modality in the agency’s Annual Procurement Plan out of its total procurements, including provisions for foreseeable emergencies based on historical records;
Issue resolutions relating to procurements to be made under Direct Retail Purchase and designate authority to specific officials for the conduct of said modality, as situations may arise; and
Observe the rest of the guidelines set forth in the RA No. 9184, in the procurement of goods and services, particularly in fuel, oil and lubricants.

We recommended that Management observe the modes of procurement provided under the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 even in transactions which are immediately necessary. All procurement activities must be in accordance with the specific law, rules and regulations.

We recommended that Management observe the modes of procurement provided under the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and other issuances of the GPPB even in transactions which are immediately necessary during a state of calamity. All procurement activities must be in accordance with the specific law, rules and regulations.

We recommended that Management procure supplies, materials and equipment directly from the PS or secure the CNAS before procuring from local suppliers. For items not available from the PS, as shown on its website, printed form of the webpage or a screenshot of the same showing the date and the time it was accessed must be secured first before procuring the CSE items from other sources.
We recommended that Management grant honoraria to BAC members based on successfully completed procurement projects following the rates provided under DBM Budget Circular 2004-5A.

We recommended that Management observe the provisions of EO No. 77 and DILG MC No. 2019-82 in the approval of official local travels and the Municipal Accountant ensure the completeness and propriety of supporting documents attached to claims for travelling expenses.

We recommended that the Office of the Municipal Accountant submit first notice of compliance to the Office of the Auditor for the issuance of NSSDC before dropping the recorded disallowances from the books of accounts. For amounts already credited, submit notice of compliance to the Office of the Auditor for the issuance of NSSDC and for the reconciliation of records.
We recommended that management explain the noted deficiencies and submit the lacking supporting documents to support the claims of terminal leave.

We recommend that in cases where requests for advance payment of 15% mobilization on infrastructure projects are made, the concerned contractors provide an irrevocable letter of credit or equivalent value from a commercial bank, bank guarantee or a surety bond callable upon demand issued by a surety or insurance company duly licensed by the Insurance Commission and confirmed by the procuring entity.

1.) Certification from concerned agency where exposure to hazard exist; and 

2.) Special Order where the claim is only equivalent to the actual period of work or co-terminus with the duration of the actual assignment of the official or employee.

We recommended that in the formulation of PPAs to be funded by the 20% Development Fund, the PPAs should be specific or with sufficient detail, so that no further authorization is required, the appropriation ordinance already being sufficient.  In the case of the identified PPAs in generic terms, specific approval by the Sanggunian is required during its implementation.

We recommended that the agency prepare and submit the Monthly Report on Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (Annex B) for evaluation and verification within the prescribed period set.

1.) Use NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No. 2013-1 as a guide in the formulation of the LDRRMFIP PPAs using the four (4) thematic areas as mandated in R.A. No. 10121; and 

2.) Review and update regularly the LDRRMFIP to consider current vulnerabilities that may arise.

We recommended that the GAD Focal Point System require the Heads of the implementing offices to ensure that GAD PPAs are implemented as planned to attain the GAD objectives; and maximize the utilization of the GAD funds through the implementation of GAD-related programs and projects on order to attain the objective for which the funds were provided.

We recommended to the management to strictly follow the JC No. 1, s. 2017 of the DepEd, DBM and DILG in the preparation and presentation of SEF budget using SEF Budget Preparation Form No. 1 (Annex A) and based it on the DepEd-approved SIP and DEDP in the implementation of ECCD Program, kindergarten, elementary and secondary, formal and non-formal education programs.
We recommended management to submit schedule and/or breakdown of the continuing appropriations as reflected in the monthly utilization report of the LDRRMF.

In view of the foregoing observations noted above, we recommended that the GAD Focal Point System:  Strictly comply with the provisions of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA and other applicable rulings relative to the submission of the approved GAD Plan and Budget and the preparation of the Annual GAD Accomplishment Reports to be approved by the different offices concerned and expenditures should be limited to the amount approved in the GAD Plan and Budget to ensure that all planned projects and activities are implemented.

We recommended the Management to require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the balances of the Cash in Vault and Cash in Bank accounts in their respective books, verify any unreconciled balances for all funds and to prepare and effect the necessary adjustments to reconcile the balances per Cashbooks and SLs.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer observe Section 59 (c) of Local Treasury Operations Manual particularly the following control measures:


Stamping serial numbers or the name or logo of the Municipality on the cash tickets before issuance;

Affixing of the initials of the collector and date of issuance in the cash tickets before issuance; and

Tearing off of cash tickets after collections has been received to avoid possible re-issuance.

We recommended the following:  (a) The RCC turn-over and remit the collections intact to the Liquidating Officer at the close of the transaction day; (b) The Municipal Treasurer require the RCC to remit collections intact as soon as the cash tickets are consumed before issuing new cash tickets in order to avoid teeming and lading (lapping); and (c) The Municipal Treasurer improve or implement measures on the present controls put in place regarding the issuance of accountable forms especially in the release of cash tickets.

We recommended the following actions to be taken by management: (a) Immediate discontinuance in the grant of additional cash advance to an officer or any official or employee unless the previous cash advance given is settled first; (b) Subsequent grant of cash advance be made only upon certification of the Accountant that the previous cash advance have been liquidated or settled and accounted in the books; (c) Strict monitoring in the submission of liquidation documents to ensure that the required number of days to liquidate will be observed; and (d) Enforcement of corresponding sanctions in the event that an accountable officer or any official or employee fails to liquidated his cash advance within the prescribed period.

We recommended to management the following:  (a) The Local Development Council monitor the implementation of the projects as incorporated in the Annual Investment Plan; (b) The Municipal Engineer coordinate with the departments concerned to fast track the implementation of the projects; (c) The Municipal Mayor shall ensure compliance with the responsibilities provided for in paragraph 5.0 of DILG and DBM Joint Circular No. 2017-1 to achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes; and (d) Incorporate projects in the Annual Invest Plan that is within the scope of allowable as identified in social development, economic development and environmental management.

We recommended to the management to srictly follow the JC No. 1, s. 2017 of the DepEd, DBM and DILG in the preparation and presentation of SEF budget using SEF Budget Preparation Form No. 1 (Annex A) and based it on the DepEd-approved SIP and DEDP in the implementation of ECCD Program, kindergarten, elementary and secondary, formal and non-formal education programs.
We recommended management that submit schedule and/or breakdown of the continuing appropriations as reflected in the monthly utilization report of the LDRRMF.

We recommended management to:  (a) ensure that all programs, projects, and activities to be incorporated in the development of the LDRRM Plan shall be responsive to the objective of the program so as to achieve full implementation; (b) conduct a thorough analysis and submit justification on the reprogrammed unutilized balance of LDRRMF for CY 2014-2017; and (c) submit schedule and/or breakdown of the continuing appropriations as reflected in the monthly utilization report of the LDRRMF.

In view of the foregoing observations noted above, we recommended that the GAD Focal Point System:  Strictly comply with the provisions of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA and other applicable rulings relative to the submission of the approved GAD Plan and Budget and the preparation of the Annual GAD Accomplishment Reports to be approved by the different offices concerned and expenditures should be limited to the amount approved in the GAD Plan and Budget to ensure that all planned projects and activities are implemented.

In view of the foregoing observations noted above, we recommended that the GAD Focal Point System: Require the GAD Focal Point System to: a. institutionalize the GAD database with adequate and systematically gathered sex-disaggregated data which will provide the bases for gender analysis, planning, programming and policy formulation; b. undertake gender analysis to identify and confirm existing gender issues and ensure that the programs, projects and activities are responsive to the said issues; and c. revisit Annex B of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 which identifies the data requirements for particular gender issues.

In view of the foregoing observations noted above, we recommended that the GAD Focal Point System: Establish a GAD monitoring and evaluation system as required under Section 3.4 and 5.1 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.

We recommended the Management to require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the balances of the Cash in Vault and Cash in Bank accounts in their respective books, verify any unreconciled balances for all funds and to prepare and effect the necessary adjustments to reconcile the balances per Cashbooks and SLs.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer observe Section 59 (c) of Local Treasury Operations Manual particularly the following control measures: Stampings serial numbers or the name or logo of the Municipality on the cash tickets before issuance; Affixing of the initials of the collector and date of issuance in the cash tickets before issuance; and Tearing off of cash tickets after collections has been received to avoid possible re-issuance.

 cash advances to  Accountable  Officer  with  previous  unliquidated  cash

 10,  1997;  3.)  Demand  the  concerned  officials  and   employees   with

 advances, otherwise, the necessary sanction for its settlement as provided
 for in paragraph 9.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-004 dated November 28,  2012
 shall be enforced; and 4.) Comply with the provisions of COA Circular  No.
 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 to  file  for  request  of  write-off  on
 dormant accounts, where appropriate.

2.8 We recommended the following:  a.) The Office of the  Municipal  Mayor
 to make representation to the  Sangguniang  Bayan  to  pass  a  resolution

 Unit to prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement of the PNB accounts  and
 make the necessary adjustments, as it warrants;  and  c.)  The  Accounting
 Unit and the Office of the Municipal Treasurer to undertake the  necessary
 reconciliation of records.

3.9 We recommended management the following:  a.) The Accounting  Unit  to
 make the necessary adjusting entry to correct the erroneous  entries  made
 and submit the same to the auditor concerned;  and  b.)  Ensure  that  the
 recognition of subsequent accounts payable is based on  the  principle  of
 receipt of goods or services rendered and not on the  approved  Obligation
 Request (OBR).

4.8 We recommended the  following:  1.)  Comply  with  the  provisions  of
 Section 111 (1) and (2) with regard to  recording  of  the  accounts;  2.)
 Verify, review and analyze the dormant Due from NGAs account  as  required
 under COA Circular No. 97-001 and effect  the  necessary  adjustment/s  to
 arrive at the correct account balances at year-end; 3.) Be guided  by  the
 procedures for write-off of dormant accounts as set forth in COA  Circular
 No. 2016-005; and 4.) Provide adequate disclosure of the  account  in  the
 Notes to the Financial Statements.

5.7 We recommended that management: 1.) Create an inventory  committee  to
 conduct a physical count of the properties of the municipality and  submit
 the Inventory Report to the Municipal Accountant and to the Office of  the
 Auditor; 2.) Designate property custodians in each  department/section  to
 initially conduct the physical count of  the  property  and  equipment  to
 facilitate the complete inventory of all  PPE  of  the  municipality;  3.)
 Municipal Accountant and  GSO/Municipal  Treasurer  maintain  ledgers  and
 stock and property cards, respectively. Also, maintain a  Property,  Plant
 and Equipment Register to facilitate  the  conduct  of  year-end  physical
 inventory, the  municipality   use  this  as  basis  in  the  conduct  and
 reconciliation of the accounts; and 4.) Submit a copy of the RPCPPE to the
 Office of the Auditor not later than January 31st of each year as provided
 in Volume II of the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) to  facilitate
 verification of the inventory report.

6.5   We  recommended  that  the   Municipal   Accountant:    1.)   Set-up
 depreciation expense for the corresponding years  where  depreciation  was
 not recognized and draw the corresponding JEV to effect the adjustments in
 the  ensuing  year;  and  2.)  Maintain  PPELC  of  each  PPE  to   record
 acquisition, description, custody, estimated  useful  life,  depreciation,
 disposal and other information about the PPE.

7.9 We recommended the following: 1.) The Accounting Unit to draw a JEV to
 make the necessary adjustment relative to the income that should have been
 recognized in CY 2019; 2.)  The  Office  of  the  Municipal  Treasurer  to
 formulate an action plan in order for the RCDs  to  be  forwarded  to  the
 Accounting Unit in a timely manner so as to recognize income in the period
 which it relates; and 3.) The Accounting Unit to ensure that at the  close
 of the year, all collections  made  during  the  year  shall  be  recorded
 accordingly.

8.11  We  recommended  the  following:  1.)  Enact  a  Sangguniang   Bayan
 Resolution    to    set     eligibility     criteria     for     qualified
 recipients/beneficiaries and  corresponding  amounts  for  each  class  of
 financial assistance; 2.)  Completeness  of  documentary  requirements  be
 complied as a  condition  precedent  in  the  processing  and  release  of
 assistance to the client; 3.) Provide a database system  for  verification
 if possible; and  4.)  Partnerships  with  partner  stakeholders  such  as
 hospitals, drugstores/pharmacies, transport companies, funeral parlors and
 other service providers shall be established by the  LGU  to  ensure  that
 referred clients are able to  receive  the  needed  services  immediately.
 Contract/s or Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) may be entered into by the LGU
 with qualified service providers in accordance with pertinent  regulations
 by providing Guarantee Letters.

9.5 We recommended that management  explain  the  noted  deficiencies  and
 submit the lacking supporting documents to support the claims of  terminal
 leave.

10.5  We  recommended  the  following:  1.)  Comply  with   the   complete
 documentary requirement in the payment of monetization  of  leave  credits
 pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-001; 2.) In  cases,  where  the  applied
 monetization for leave is more than ten (10) days, secure a  request  duly
 approved by the Head of the Agency which should not to exceed thirty  (30)
 days in a given year; 3.) The monetized leave should be deducted from  the
 accumulated Vacation Leave credits where at least five (5) days  shall  be
 retained after monetization; and 4.) Comply with Section 23 of the Omnibus
 Rules on Leave when circumstance arises that monetization of 50 percent or
 more of accumulated leave credits is requested.

11.4 We recommended that in cases where requests for  advance  payment  of
 15% mobilization  on  infrastructure  projects  are  made,  the  concerned
 contractors provide an irrevocable letter of credit  or  equivalent  value
 from a commercial bank, bank guarantee or  a  surety  bond  callable  upon
 demand issued by a surety  or  insurance  company  duly  licensed  by  the
 Insurance Commission and confirmed by the procuring entity.

12.4 We recommended the submission of  the  following:  1.)  Certification
 from concerned agency where exposure to  hazard  exist;  and  2.)  Special
 Order where the claim is only equivalent to the actual period of  work  or
 co-terminus with the duration of the actual assignment of the official  or
 employee.

13.3 We recommended that management:  1.)  Provide  notice  of  deliveries
 within twenty four (24) hours from deliveries  of  goods/items  delivered;
 and 2.) Submit Report of Inspection of Consumables within twenty four (24)
 hours.

14.4 We recommended that in the formulation of PPAs to be  funded  by  the
 20% Development Fund, the  PPAs should  be  specific  or  with  sufficient
 detail, so that no further authorization is  required,  the  appropriation
 ordinance already being sufficient.  In the case of the identified PPAs in
 generic terms, specific approval by the Sanggunian is required during  its
 implementation.

15.3 We recommended that the agency prepare and submit the Monthly  Report
 on Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management  Fund  (Annex  B)
 for evaluation and verification within the prescribed period set.

16.3 We recommended that management implement optimally the PPAs contained
 in the LDRRMF Investment Plan.

17.4 We recommended the following: 1.) Use  NDRRMC,  DBM  and  DILG  Joint
 Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2013-1 as a guide in the formulation of  the
 LDRRMFIP PPAs using  the four (4) thematic areas as mandated in  R.A.  No.
 10121; and 2.) Review  and  update  regularly  the  LDRRMFIP  to  consider
 current vulnerabilities that may arise.

18.4 We recommended that the GAD Focal Point System  require  the  Heads  of
the implementing offices to ensure that GAD PPAs are implemented as  planned
to attain the GAD objectives; and maximize the utilization of the GAD  funds
through the implementation of GAD-related programs and projects on order  to
attain the objective for which the funds were provided.

We recommended to management the following: (a) Prepare monthly the Bank Reconciliation Statements and submit the same within the reglementary period prescribed under COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 together with its supporting documents such as Monthly Bank Statement Snapshot to facilitate the early submission of the BRS, Subsidiary Ledgers, paid checks, debit and credit memos and deposit slips; and (b) Prepare the necessary adjusting entries for any reconciling items noted in the process.

We recommended that agency increase the bond of the RCC (Collecting        Officer) in order to cover in cases of defalcation, shortages and unrelieved loss from the Maximum Cash Accountability of P100,000.00 to P500,000.00 as provided in Annex C of TC No. 02-2009 Circular provides for the Schedule of Premium Rates for Cash, Property and Accountable Forms.

We recommended management that transfer of funds from one account to another or from one fund to another be accompanied with an approved resolution from the Sangguniang Bayan authorizing such transfer.
We recommended that share in the proceeds of the Community Tax and Real Property Tax due to the barangay be released within the prescribed period provided for under R.A. 7160.
We recommended to management the following: (a) Only authorized deductions be allowed in order of preference; and (b) Ensure that an employee receives the required net take home pay mandated by law.

We recommended that management:  (a) strictly follow the limitation guidelines in appropriating for discretionary fund and extraordinary expenses as provided under Section 325 (h) of R.A. 7160 of two percent (2%) of the actual receipts derived from basic real property tax in the next preceding year; and (b) Increase the collection of the real property tax in order to increase appropriation of the discretionary fund.

We recommended the management to: (a) ensure that subsequent procurement of common-use supplies and equipment should be done thru the Procurement Service, in compliance with R.A. No. 9184 and Administrative Order No. 17, series of 2011; and (b) in cases where the requested items are not available in the PS-Depot, secure a certification from PS-DBM that such items are not available.

We recommended management to: (a) conduct property tagging on all farm equipment distributed to different barangays under PAMANA Phase III with the coordination of PAMANA Focal Person and the Property Unit; and (b) conduct of assessment on the impact of the program taking into consideration the projects that remained idle/unutilized despite its completion and submit the results thereafter.
We recommended to the management the following: spend within the allowable expenses chargeable   report of the SEF Budget.
We recommended to management to cause additional transfer of funds to LDRRMF Trust Fund account to offset the insufficiency.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant comply with the provisions of Section I, Rule VI of the IRR of R.A. No. 9679 on the amount of premiums for government share to be remitted to the Pag-ibig Fund.

We recommended to Management the following: a.) Direct the Focal Person and Municipal Engineer to judiciously prepare and submit project documents to expedite fund releases, implementation and completion of PAMANA projects; b.) The BAC should fast-track the procurement process in consonance with the earliest mandated time prescribed in the IRR of RA 9184; c.) Direct the Focal Person and Municipal Engineer to constantly monitor project implementation to ensure that projects were implemented in accordance with the government standards and MOA; and d.) Direct the Focal Person, Municipal Engineer and Municipal Accountant to regularly prepare and submit SORD and liquidation documents to DA to ensure prompt release of subsequent tranches and reconcile both accounting records.

observations.recommendation: We recommend to management the following: (a) Prepare monthly the Bank Reconciliation Statements and submit the same within the reglementary period prescribed under COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 together with its supporting documents such as Monthly Bank Statement Snapshot to facilitate the early submission of the BRS, Subsidiary Ledgers, paid checks, debit and credit memos and deposit slips; and (b) Prepare the necessary adjusting entries for any reconciling items noted in the process.

We recommend to management the following: (a) refrain from granting additional cash advances to Accountable Officer with previous unliquidated cash advances; (b)   enforce full liquidation of cash advances within the reglementary period provided under COA Circular No.97-002 dated February 10, 1997; (c) demand the concerned officials and employees with unliquidated cash advances to effect immediate settlement of cash advances, otherwise, the necessary sanction for its settlement as provided for in paragraph 9.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-004 dated November 28, 2012 shall be enforced; and (d) comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 to file for request of write-off on dormant accounts.

We recommend that agency increase the bond of the RCC (Collecting Officer) in order to cover in cases of defalcation, shortages and unrelieved loss from the Maximum Cash Accountability of P100,000.00 to P500,000.00 as provided in Annex C of TC No. 02-2009 Circular provides for the Schedule of Premium Rates for Cash, Property and Accountable Forms.

We recommend the following:  (a) The RCC turn-over and remit the collections intact to the Liquidating Officer at the close of the transaction day; (b) The Municipal Treasurer require the RCC to remit collections intact as soon as the cash tickets are consumed before issuing new cash tickets in order to avoid teeming and lading (lapping); and (c) The Municipal Treasurer improve or implement measures on the present controls put in place regarding the issuance of accountable forms especially in the release of cash tickets.

We recommend the following actions to be taken by management: (a) Immediate discontinuance in the grant of additional cash advance to an officer or any official or employee unless the previous cash advance given is settled first; (b) Subsequent grant of cash advance be made only upon certification of the Accountant that the previous cash advance have been liquidated or settled and accounted in the books; (c) Strict monitoring in the submission of liquidation documents to ensure that the required number of days to liquidate will be observed; and (d) Enforcement of corresponding sanctions in the event that an accountable officer or any official or employee fails to liquidated his cash advance within the prescribed period.
We recommend management that transfer of funds from one account to another or from one fund to another be accompanied with an approved resolution from the Sangguniang Bayan authorizing such transfer.

We recommend that management:  (a) Creation of an Inventory Committee to conduct a physical count of the properties of the municipality; (b) Maintenance of Property, Plant and Equipment Register to facilitate the conduct of year-end physical inventory, the municipality  use this as basis in the conduct and reconciliation of the accounts; and (c) Submission of RPCPPE to the Office of the Auditor not later than January 31st of each year as provided in Volume II of the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) to facilitate verification of the inventory report.
We recommend that share in the proceeds of the Community Tax and Real Property Tax due to the barangay be released within the prescribed period provided for under R.A. 7160.
We recommend to management the following: (a) Only authorized deductions be allowed in order of preference; and (b) Ensure that an employee receives the required net take home pay mandated by law.

We recommend that the agency comply with the provisions of Section 16 of the IRR of R.A. 9433, to wit: (a) Certification from concerned agency where exposure to hazard exist; and (b) Special Order where the claim is only equivalent to the actual period of work or co-terminus with the duration of the actual assignment of the official or employee.

We recommend that management:  (a) strictly follow the limitation guidelines in appropriating for discretionary fund and extraordinary expenses as provided under Section 325 (h) of R.A. 7160 of two percent (2%) of the actual receipts derived from basic real property tax in the next preceding year; and (b) Increase the collection of the real property tax in order to increase appropriation of the discretionary fund.

We recommend that management: (a) Set up policies or guidelines in the evaluation of requests for grants or donations for the promotion of public welfare; (b) Require the recipient/grantee to account for the grant received for which the purpose of the grant was given;  (c) Adhere strictly to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2007-001 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 in the grant of financial assistance to NGOs/POs and ensure that the supporting documents are complete and attached before donations to such groups are approved; and (d) Create a committee to screen and evaluate the qualifications of the NGOs/POs and their advocacies/commitments vis-à-vis the provisions of the cited regulations and following a rational financial assistance plan comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2007-001 in the grant of donations for NGOs/POs,

We recommend the management to: (a) ensure that subsequent procurement of common-use supplies and equipment should be done thru the Procurement Service, in compliance with R.A. No. 9184 and Administrative Order No. 17, series of 2011; and (b) in cases where the requested items are not available in the PS-Depot, secure a certification from PS-DBM that such items are not available.

We recommend to management the following:  (a) The Local Development Council monitor the implementation of the projects as incorporated in the Annual Investment Plan; (b) The Municipal Engineer coordinate with the departments concerned to fast track the implementation of the projects; (c)  The Municipal Mayor shall ensure compliance with the responsibilities provided for in paragraph 5.0 of DILG and DBM Joint Circular No. 2017-1 to achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes; and (d)  Incorporate projects in the Annual Invest Plan that is within the scope of allowable as identified in social development, economic development and environmental management.

We recommend management to: (a) conduct property tagging on all farm equipment distributed to different barangays under PAMANA Phase III with the coordination of PAMANA Focal Person and the Property Unit; and (b) conduct of assessment on the impact of the program taking into consideration the projects that remained idle/unutilized despite its completion and submit the results thereafter.

We recommend to the management the following:  (a) Strictly follow the JC No. 1, s. 2017 of the DepEd, DBM and DILG in the preparation and presentation of SEF budget using SEF Budget Preparation Form No. 1 (Annex A) and based it on the DepEd-approved SIP and DEDP in the implementation of ECCD Program, kindergarten, elementary and secondary, formal and non-formal education programs; (b) Post the utilization report of the SEF Budget in the website of the LGU and/or in at least three (3) conspicuous public places for transparency and accountability in compliance with the Full Disclosure Policy of the DILG; (c) Furnish COA with copies of the Quarterly and Annual Reports of SEF Utilization, as prepared by the Local Accountant, received by DepEd, the local sanggunian concerned and Regional Offices of the DBM and DILG; and (d) The in the preparation of the Local School Board Budget, the members of the Local School Board give priority to programs, activities and projects based in the DepEd-approved SIP and DEDP in the implementation of the ECCD Program, kindergarten, elementary and secondary, formal and non-formal education programs  and utilize the same to such purpose prescribed by law for which the SEF was created.
We recommend management to cause additional transfer of funds to LDRRMF Trust Fund account to offset the insufficiency.

We recommend management to:  (a) ensure that all programs, projects, and activities to be incorporated in the development of the LDRRM Plan shall be responsive to the objective of the program so as to achieve full implementation; (b) conduct a thorough analysis and submit justification on the reprogrammed unutilized balance of LDRRMF for CY 2014-2017; and (c) submit schedule and/or breakdown of the continuing appropriations as reflected in the monthly utilization report of the LDRRMF.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant comply with the provisions of Section I, Rule VI of the IRR of R.A. No. 9679 on the amount of premiums for government share to be remitted to the Pag-ibig Fund.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations

      We recommend the  following  measures  in  order  to  prevent  further
accumulation of outstanding cash advances:


      Direct the Municipal Accountant to:


         • Submit to the Audit Team Leader (ATL) the  detailed  listing  of
           the employees  and  officials  who  consisted  the  unliquidated
           balance  as  of  September  30,  2017   of   ₱14,334,897.40   by
           accomplishing Annex A to D of COA Memo 2017-010.


         •   Submit to  the  ATL  the  demand  letters  for  the  immediate
           liquidation of overdue  cash  advances,  duly  received  by  the
           AO/employee.


         • Refrain from granting additional cash  advances  to  AO/employee
           with previous unliquidated cash advances.
         • Comply with the documentary requirements, more so  with  regards
           to  the  Certification  of   the   Accountant   concerning   the
           liquidation of previous cash advances.
         • For dormant unliquidated cash advance, request for write off  in
           accordance with COA Circular No.  2016-005  dated  December  18,
           2016.

Recommendations

      We recommend the following:


        1.  The  Inventory  Committee  shall  conduct   complete   physical
           inventory of all property and reconcile results with  accounting
           records and require  the  Municipal  Accountant  to  record  any
           adjustments in the books.  This  includes  the  results  of  the
           inventory and recognition of the local road networks within  the
           Municipality and  submit  the  results  thereof  to  the  Agency
           Auditor;

        2. The  Municipal  Treasurer/Property  officer  shall  prepare  the
           Inventory and Inspection Report on all unserviceable  properties
           that will be identified during the count  and  facilitate  their
           immediate disposal to prevent further deterioration and decrease
           in salvage values; and

        3. The Municipal  Accountant  shall  provide  depreciation  on  all
           depreciable assets.

Recommendation

      We recommend that management require collectors to turnover  or  remit
their collections daily and intact to the liquidating officer  to  safeguard
government funds from possible misappropriation, misapplication or loss.

Management Comment

      The Municipal Treasurer committed to enforce the recommendation.

Recommendation

      We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer secure a  DOF  approval  for
its deposit and maintenance of account  with  PNB  in  compliance  with  DOF
Department Circular No.  002-2016. Otherwise, close the account and  transfer
it to any of the authorized government depository banks.

Recommendation

      We recommend the Management to require the  Municipal  Accountant  and
the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the balances of the Cash in  Vault  and
Cash in Bank accounts in their respective  books,  verify  any  unreconciled
balances for all funds and to prepare and effect the  necessary  adjustments
to reconcile the balances per Cashbooks and SLs.

Recommendation


      We recommend the Field Collecting Officer to  prepare  the  Report  of
Collection and Deposit and work on the preparation and maintenance of the
Cashbook – Cash in Treasury for his collection to  facilitate  verification,
monitoring and reconciliation of  Cash  in  Treasury  balances  pursuant  to
Sections 60 (A) and 63 (C) of  the Local Treasury Operations Manual.

Management Comment


      The Municipal Treasurer explained that their  FCO  already  maintained
individual RCD and Cash Book - Cash in Treasury for their collections as  of
December 31, 2017.

Recommendation


      We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer to certify in the  Cashbooks
– Cash in Bank as to  the  correctness  and  completeness  of  the  recorded
transactions at month-end after the last entry in the cashbook.

Management Comment

      The  Municipal  Treasurer  justified  that  he  had  already   started
certifying  the  cashbooks  at  month  end  after  the   conduct   of   cash
examination.

Recommendation


      We recommend that the  Municipal  Treasurer  observe  immediately  the
Internal Control measure for cash by seeing to it that Disbursement  Voucher
and its supporting documents are  conspicuously  cancelled/  invalidated  as
“PAID” after each payment.

Recommendations

      We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer observe Section  59  (c)  of
Local  Treasury  Operations  Manual  particularly  the   following   control
measures:


         a. Stamping serial numbers or the name or logo of the  Municipality
            on the cash tickets before issuance;
   b. Affixing of the initials of the collector and date of issuance in  the
      cash tickets before issuance; and
   c. Tearing off of cash tickets after collections  has  been  received  to
      avoid possible re-issuance.

Recommendation

      We recommend the Municipal Treasurer to strictly follow  the  use  of
the RIS for an accurate utilization/issuance of Accountable Forms.

Recommendation

      We  recommend  that  the  Accountable  Officers’  fidelity  bonds   be
increased that are sufficient to recover in case of defalcations,  shortages
and unrelieved  losses  and  in  accordance  with  the  Bureau  of  Treasury
Circular No. 2-2009 dated August 6, 2009.

Recommendation

      We recommend that Management require the full and proper accounting of
the   accounts   by    providing/maintaining    subsidiary    ledgers    and
schedules/details to support the accounts  as  presented  in  the  financial
statements.

Recommendation

      We recommend that  the  Municipal  Accountant  secure  Certificate  of
Completion, and Turn over/Acceptance from  DILG  to  record  the  SalinTubig
Project Level I and  II  implemented  by  DILG,  to  the  appropriate  asset
accounts of the LGU, to comply with Section 50 of the NGAS Manual for  LGUs,
Volume I, and in  order  the  infrastructure  asset  and  government  equity
accounts of the LGU are correctly reported as of year-end.

Recommendation

    We recommend the  Accountant  to  strictly  enforce  the  provision  of
Section 62.2.3.3 of the Implementing Rules and Regulation of the  Government
Procurement  Act  by  requiring  the  Contractor  to  post  Warranty  Security  in
the procurement of Infrastructure Projects in order  to  fully  protect  the
interest of the Government in case of Structural Defects and/or  Failure  of
the completed infrastructure projects.

Recommendation

       We  recommended  that  the  ICO-Municipal  Accountant   to   strictly
observe/follow the updated description of accounts as prescribed  under  COA
Circular No. 2015-009 dated  December  1,  2015.  Also,  we  recommend  that
management submit the required documents  stated  in  Annex  A  for  further
verification of the validity of the transaction and to ensure that the  fund
were used for its intended purpose and that the claims are valid.

Recommendation


      We recommend that Management provide annual appropriation  for  Gender
and Development activities in the LGU’s Annual Budget amounting to at  least
5% of the total appropriation.

Recommendation

      We recommend that the GFPS strictly monitor the implementation of  GAD
PPAs as required under JMC No.  2016-01  and  conduct  regular  coordination
with the implementing office to ensure that plans are undertaken within  the
target period.

Recommendation

      We recommend that the provisions for warranty security under  the  IRR
of R.A. 9184 be strictly complied with to ensure guarantee for indemnity  in
case the goods delivered are found to be of defective.

Recommendations

      We recommend that the Accounting Office should:


1. Prepare a reconciliation report of the reciprocal accounts  and  take  up
   the necessary adjustment in the books of accounts of the respective funds
   to be submitted with the Agency Auditor; and
2. Instruct the Accounting personnel assigned to  journalize  the  financial
   transactions to record and set-up simultaneously the  reciprocal  account
   in the different funds affected  and  for  the  Municipal  Accountant  to
   constantly review the recording of such transactions.

We restate the previous recommendations regarding this matter that Management:

• Refrain from granting additional cash advances to Accountable Officer with previous unliquidated cash advances and withhold salaries or any amount due to the concerned officials and employees pursuant to the aforementioned provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002. Should important travel or other expenditures are to be incurred, the expenditures shall be reimbursed instead of advanced until such time that all prior cash advances are already liquidated; and
• Ensure compliance with the documentary requirements, more so with regards to the Certification of the Accountant concerning the liquidation of previous cash advances.

No. 9184 in the procurement of goods and services. Moreover, the practice of payment through reimbursement should be refrained by paying the amount due directly to the creditor.

observations.recommendation: Immediate steps be taken to attract more occupants to have enough funds to settle the loan obligation. It is necessary that unfilled market space must be occupied to increase additional income of the Municipality. Strategies to improve the operations of the Public Market include: a. develop a public plaza and a tourism information center; b. create a market day every week for fisher folk and farmers; c. encourage the vendors form the nearby barangays to sell all local products at the Public Market; d. campaign for the promotions of all local vendors; e. offer a three (3) months free of rent in the market; and f. pass an effective ordinance prohibiting all vendors located along the road right of way for reason of high risk of vehicular accidents.
observations.managementAction: Hereunder are the Strategies that the LGU implemented to improve the operations of the Public Market to wit: a. the need to develop a public plaza and a tourism information center in order to gather people together and to attract more buyer; b. create a market day every week for fisher folk and farmers; c. encourage the vendors from the nearby barangays to sell all local products at the Public Market; d. campaign for the promotions of all local vendors; e. all local vendors are welcome to make a display at the Public Market, fish or meat section; f. offer a three (3) months free of rent in the market; and g. pass an effective ordinance prohibiting all vendors located along the road right of way for reason of high risk of vehicular accidents.

observations.recommendation:
We restate  the  previous  recommendations  regarding  this
matter that Management:

 •  Demand  the  concerned  officials  and  employees  with
   unliquidated   cash   advances   to   effect   immediate
   settlement of cash advances;





 •  Refrain  from  granting  additional  cash  advances  to
   Accountable  Officer  with  previous  unliquidated  cash
   advances and withhold salaries or any amount due to  the
   concerned  officials  and  employees  pursuant  to   the
   aforementioned provisions of COA  Circular  No.  97-002.
   Should important travel or other expenditures are to  be
   incurred, the expenditures shall be  reimbursed  instead
   of advanced until such time that all prior cash advances
   are already liquidated; and




 • Ensure compliance  with  the  documentary  requirements,
   more  so  with  regards  to  the  Certification  of  the
   Accountant concerning the liquidation of  previous  cash
   advances.

We recommend the following:


   1. The Municipal  Treasurer/Property  Officer  maintain  stock
      ledger cards, property ledger cards, and  other  subsidiary
      records  and  reconcile  accounting  and  property  records
      periodically;




   2. The Inventory Committee  shall  conduct  complete  physical
      inventory  of  all  property  and  reconcile  results  with
      accounting records and effect any adjustments in the books;
      and




   3. The Municipal Treasurer/Property officer shall prepare  the
      Inventory  and  Inspection  Report  on  all   unserviceable
      properties that will be identified  during  the  count  and
      facilitate their  immediate  disposal  to  prevent  further
      deterioration and decrease in salvage values. The Municipal
      Accountant shall effect the necessary adjustment in the PPE
      Account to  reclassify  the  book  value  of  unserviceable
      properties to Other Assets  account  pending  disposal  and
      close the corresponding Accumulated Depreciation account.

the copies of Memorandum of Agreement (MOA)/ Trust Agreement  on
all funds transferred by National Government Agencies (NGAs)  as

21

observations.recommendation:
We restate the our previous recommendations that Management: 
a. Demand the concerned officials and employees with unliquidated cash advances to effect immediate settlement of cash advances;

b. Refrain from granting additional cash advances to Accountable Officer with previous unliquidated cash advances and withhold salaries or any amount due to the concerned officials and employees pursuant to the aforementioned provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002. Should important travel or other expenditures are to be incurred, the expenditures shall be reimbursed instead of advanced until such time that all prior cash advances are already liquidated; and

c. Ensure compliance with the documentary requirements, more so with regards to the Certification of the Accountant concerning the liquidation of previous cash advances.

observations.reason:
Some officials and employees of the Municipality were still allowed to avail cash advances despite the non-liquidation of their previous cash advance.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that collections and deposits should be recorded daily in the cashbooks to enable the Accountable Officers to reconcile cashbook balance with cash on hand at the close of each day.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management require the Accountable Officers/Collectors to remit/turnover their collections to the Liquidating Officer for deposit to the depository bank on a regular basis in accordance with Section 29 of the NGAS Manual. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to exercise the diligence of a good father of a family in monitoring the remittances of collections and deposits of the collecting officers. He should set control measures to avoid or minimize delayed remittance of collections by requiring Revenue Collectors to remit first the proceeds of all Official Receipts issued prior to issuances of new booklet, otherwise he (Municipal Treasurer) shall be jointly and solidarily held liable for any misappropriation or loss of government funds under his control.

observations.reason: There were still delayed in remittances of collections.
